story_name: Historical kind-1059 fetch and replay on chat mount

changes: init async function: parallel kind-1059 historical fetch added to Promise.all alongside kind-4 incoming/outgoing fetches
init async function: step 5 merges kind-4 and kind-1059 results, sorts by createdAt, calls upsertMessages once
handleHistoricalGiftWrapEvent closure: processes each gift wrap through unwrapAndOpen → shouldIngestRumor → parseDirectPayload → appendMessage

spec_compliance.merged_upsert: single upsertMessages call after both fetches resolve with sorted merged array
spec_compliance.no_new_imports: fetchEventsWithTimeout already imported, all other symbols already in scope

implementation.logging: dm:unwrap-failed (info level) emitted by existing unwrapAndOpen on failure; no new logging needed per AC-30
implementation.error_handling: try/catch in handleHistoricalGiftWrapEvent silently skips unwrap failures per spec §3.6; init's outer try/catch catches any unhandled rejection

RETROSPECTIVE.note: The vi.mock + dynamic import pattern creates a subtle mock-state isolation hazard: tests that set mockImplementation after vi.mock runs in a module-level closure can leak state across tests. The fix is to use a shared mutable state object (window-style closure) that both the mock implementation and the test setup read/write, rather than trying to control the mock via per-test mockImpl overrides. The architecture insight: for shared-state mocks in vitest, prefer 'the mock reads from shared variables' over 'the test overrides the mock'.
